data.name: Endocrine Therapy Prior to Randomization Limit
Major Surgery Washout Prior to Randomization
Physical Exam Not Repeated on Day 1 If Done Within 7 Days
Study Treatment Delay for Logistical Reasons
Unscheduled Visit Required If >40 Days Between V26 and V27
Recurrent Toxicity Window (Same Event Within 8 Weeks)
Eligibility Based on Nodal Status, Tumor Size, or Grade (Cohort 1)
data.description: Patients eligible based on nodal status, tumor size, or grade regardless of Ki-67 status
data.text: Cohort 1: eligible based on nodal status, tumor size, or grade regardless of Ki-67 status

data.name: Eligibility Based on Ki-67 Status (Cohort 2)
data.description: Patients with at least 1 positive node eligible exclusively based on Ki-67 status
data.text: Cohort 2: at least 1 positive node and eligible exclusively based on a Ki-67 status (patients not eligible based on degree of axillary lymph node involvement, tumor size or grade)

data.name: Randomization Within 16 Months of Surgery
data.description: Patients must be randomized within 16 months of definitive breast surgery
data.text: Patients must be randomized within 16 months of definitive breast surgery for the current malignancy

data.name: Endocrine Therapy Limit Prior to Randomization
data.description: Up to 12 weeks of endocrine therapy allowed prior to randomization
data.text: Patients in both arms may have up to 12 weeks of endocrine therapy following their last non-endocrine therapy (surgery, chemotherapy, or radiotherapy) prior to randomization

data.name: ECOG Performance Status
data.description: Patient must have ECOG PS ≤1
data.text: The patient has a performance status ≤1 on the Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group scale

data.name: Adequate Organ Function
data.description: Patient must have adequate organ function per defined criteria
data.text: The patient has adequate organ function for all of the following criteria

data.name: Negative Blood Pregnancy Test
data.description: Women of reproductive potential must have negative blood pregnancy test within 14 days prior to randomization
data.text: Women of reproductive potential must have a negative blood pregnancy test at baseline (within 14 days prior to randomization)

data.name: Recovery from Chemotherapy
data.description: Patients must have recovered from acute effects of chemotherapy to CTCAE Grade ≤1
data.text: Patients must have recovered (CTCAE Grade ≤1) from the acute effects of chemotherapy except for residual alopecia or Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy prior to randomization

data.name: Recovery from Radiotherapy
data.description: Patients must have recovered from acute effects of radiotherapy to Grade ≤1
data.text: Patients must have recovered (Grade ≤1) from the acute effects of radiotherapy

data.name: Significant Noncompliance Threshold
data.description: Patient repeatedly misses more than 25% of abemaciclib doses or takes ≥125% of prescribed amount
data.text: A patient will be considered significantly noncompliant if she or he repeatedly misses more than 25% of abemaciclib doses between scheduled clinic visits. Similarly, a patient will be considered significantly noncompliant if she or he is judged by the investigator to have intentionally or repeatedly taken more than (≥125%) the prescribed amount of medication.

data.name: ALT/AST Grade 4 Discontinuation Trigger
data.description: ALT/AST increased Grade 4 (>20.0×ULN) requires abemaciclib discontinuation
data.text: ALT/AST Increased Grade 4 (>20.0×ULN): Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued

data.name: Hy's Law Criteria Discontinuation Trigger
data.description: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3×ULN with total bilirubin >2×ULN in absence of cholestasis
data.text: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3 x ULN with total bilirubin >2 x ULN, in the absence of cholestasis: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued

data.name: ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3-4 Discontinuation Trigger
data.description: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4 requires abemaciclib discontinuation
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued

data.name: Minimum Dose Level Reached
data.description: Patient on 50 mg twice daily requiring further dose reduction must discontinue abemaciclib
data.text: If a patient receiving the 50-mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction, the patient must be discontinued from abemaciclib

data.name: IDFS Event per STEEP Criteria
data.description: Patient experiences any IDFS event as per STEEP criteria
data.text: The patient experiences any of the IDFS events as per STEEP criteria

data.name: Unscheduled Visit Required Between V26 and V27
data.description: Unscheduled visits must be scheduled if >40 days between Visit 26 and Visit 27
data.text: Unscheduled visits must be scheduled to complete the 2-year period between Visit 26 and Visit 27 if there are >40 days between Visit 26 and Visit 27

data.name: Diarrhea Grade 2 Not Resolving Within 24 Hours
data.description: Diarrhea Grade 2 that does not resolve within 24 hours to at least Grade 1 triggers dose suspension
data.text: Diarrhea Grade 2 that does not resolve within 24 hours to at least Grade 1: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 1

data.name: Non-Hematologic Toxicity Persistent Grade 2 Not Resolving Within 7 Days
data.description: Persistent or recurrent Grade 2 non-hematologic toxicity not resolving with maximal supportive measures within 7 days
data.text: Persistent or recurrent Grade 2 that does not resolve with maximal supportive measures within 7 days to baseline or Grade 1, or Grade 3 or 4: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to either baseline or Grade 1

data.name: Screening to On-Study Treatment Transition
data.text: Patient must meet all inclusion criteria and no exclusion criteria. First dose of study treatment should be taken no later than 3 days after randomization. Eligibility must be based on the last available screening laboratory results prior to randomization.

data.name: On-Study Treatment to Short-Term Follow-Up Transition
data.text: Short-term follow-up visit takes place 30 days (±5 days) after completion of the 2-year on-study treatment period or after discontinuation criteria are met and decision is made for the patient to discontinue all study treatment, whichever occurs first.

data.name: Short-Term Follow-Up to Long-Term Follow-Up Transition
data.text: The long-term follow-up period begins the day after the short-term follow-up visit. ALL patients will enter the long-term follow-up period.

data.name: Discontinuation of Abemaciclib for ALT/AST Grade 4
data.text: ALT/AST Increased Grade 4 (>20.0×ULN): Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation of Abemaciclib for Hy's Law
data.text: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3×ULN with total bilirubin >2×ULN, in the absence of cholestasis: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation of Abemaciclib for ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3-4
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4: Abemaciclib therapy MUST be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation of Abemaciclib at Minimum Dose Level
data.text: If a patient receiving the 50-mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction, the patient must be discontinued from abemaciclib.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Hematologic Toxicity Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4
data.text: Recurrent Grade 3 or Grade 4 hematologic toxicity: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Growth Factor Use
data.text: If patient requires administration of blood cell growth factors: Dose MUST be suspended for at least 48 hours after the last dose of growth factors and until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level unless already performed.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Non-Hematologic Toxicity
data.text: Persistent or recurrent Grade 2 non-hematologic toxicity (except diarrhea, ALT/AST, ILD/pneumonitis, VTE) not resolving within 7 days, or Grade 3 or 4: Dose MUST be suspended until resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Dose Suspension for Diarrhea Grade 2
data.text: Diarrhea Grade 2 that does not resolve within 24 hours to at least Grade 1: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 1. Dose reduction is NOT required.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Persistent/Recurrent Diarrhea or Grade 3-4
data.text: Persistent or recurrent Grade 2 diarrhea that does not resolve with maximal supportive measures, or requires hospitalization, or Grade 3 or 4: Dose MUST be suspended until resolves to at least Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Dose Reduction for ALT/AST Grade 2-3
data.text: Persistent or recurrent ALT/AST Grade 2 (>3.0-5.0×ULN) or Grade 3 (>5.0-20.0×ULN): Dose MUST be suspended until resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Dose Reduction for ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 2
data.text: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 2: Dose MUST be suspended until resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Dose MUST be reduced by 1 dose level.

data.name: Dose Suspension for Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3
data.text: Hematologic Toxicity Grade 3: Dose MUST be suspended until toxicity resolves to at least Grade 2. Dose reduction is NOT required.

data.name: Discontinuation for IDFS Event
data.text: The patient experiences any of the IDFS events as per STEEP criteria: study treatment must be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for Significant Noncompliance
data.text: Patients who are significantly noncompliant with abemaciclib may be discontinued from abemaciclib treatment. Significant noncompliance defined as repeatedly missing more than 25% of doses or taking ≥125% of prescribed amount.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: The patient becomes pregnant during the study: study treatment must be discontinued.

data.name: Discontinuation for Unacceptable Toxicity
data.text: The patient experiences unacceptable toxicity: study treatment must be discontinued.

data.name: Partial Discontinuation in Arm A - Continue Other Drug
data.text: For Arm A only, if a patient discontinues only one of the combination drugs (abemaciclib or endocrine therapy) prior to completion of the 2-year on-study treatment period for a reason other than an IDFS event per STEEP criteria, she/he should continue the other drug until completion of the 2-year on-study treatment period or other discontinuation criteria are met, whichever occurs first.

data.name: Dose Suspension >28 Days Requires Lilly Consultation
data.text: Dose suspensions ≥28 days must be discussed with Lilly CRP/CRS. For patients not recovering from toxicity within 28 days, a delay >28 days is permitted upon agreement between the investigator and the Lilly CRP/CRS and abemaciclib dose adjustment is to be considered.

data.name: Early Termination - IDFS Event
data.description: Patient experiences any IDFS event as per STEEP criteria, requiring discontinuation of study treatment. Patient remains in study for follow-up.

data.name: Early Termination - Unacceptable Toxicity
data.description: Patient experiences unacceptable toxicity requiring discontinuation of study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination - Patient Decision
data.description: The patient requests to be withdrawn from the study or study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination - Investigator Decision
data.description: The investigator decides that the patient should be discontinued from the study or study treatment.

data.name: Early Termination - Sponsor Decision
data.description: Lilly stops the study or stops the patient's participation in the study for medical, safety, regulatory, or other reasons consistent with applicable laws, regulations, and GCP.

data.name: Early Termination - Pregnancy
data.description: The patient becomes pregnant during the study.

data.name: Early Termination - Significant Noncompliance
data.description: The patient is significantly noncompliant with study procedures and/or treatment.

data.name: Early Termination - Enrollment in Incompatible Study
data.description: The patient is enrolled in any other clinical trial involving an investigational product or any other type of medical research judged not to be scientifically or medically compatible with this study.

data.name: Early Termination - ALT/AST Grade 4
data.description: ALT/AST Increased Grade 4 (>20.0×ULN) requires mandatory abemaciclib discontinuation.

data.name: Early Termination - Hy's Law
data.description: Elevation in AST and/or ALT >3×ULN with total bilirubin >2×ULN in the absence of cholestasis requires mandatory abemaciclib discontinuation.

data.name: Early Termination - ILD/Pneumonitis Grade 3-4
data.description: ILD/pneumonitis Grade 3 or Grade 4 requires mandatory abemaciclib discontinuation.

data.name: Early Termination - Minimum Dose Requiring Further Reduction
data.description: Patient receiving 50 mg twice daily dose of abemaciclib requires further dose reduction and must be discontinued from abemaciclib.

data.name: Study Completion
data.description: Completion of the 2-year on-study treatment period followed by short-term follow-up visit and long-term follow-up through Year 10.

data.name: Dose Modification Decision at Each Visit
data.description: At each clinic visit, assess toxicity and determine if dose suspension, dose reduction, or discontinuation of abemaciclib is required per Table JPCF.7.2 dose adjustment guidelines.

data.name: Visit 27 Scheduling Decision
data.description: Decision point to determine if unscheduled visits are needed between Visit 26 and Visit 27 if gap exceeds 40 days. Visit 27 must be anchored to Visit 1 at 24 months (±5 days).

data.name: End of Treatment Period Decision
data.description: At completion of 2-year on-study treatment period, transition patient to short-term follow-up. If discontinuation criteria met earlier, transition occurs at that time.

data.name: Arm A Partial Discontinuation Decision
data.description: If Arm A patient discontinues one drug (abemaciclib or endocrine therapy) for reason other than IDFS event, continue the other drug until 2-year completion or other discontinuation criteria met.

data.name: Noncompliance Assessment Decision
data.description: At each clinic visit, assess treatment compliance by reconciliation. If patient repeatedly misses >25% of doses or takes ≥125% of prescribed amount, consider discontinuation from abemaciclib.

data.name: Dose Suspension Duration Decision
data.description: If dose suspension reaches 28 days without recovery from toxicity, investigator must discuss with Lilly CRP/CRS. Delay >28 days permitted upon agreement and dose adjustment to be considered.
